summary: Recreation center attendance was recovering from pandemic-related closures across this period: NYC Parks' own reporting says FY25's total of over 2 million attendees was the first time that mark was surpassed "since before pandemic related recreation center closures in Fiscal 2020," which explains most of the rise from FY22's low base. The subsequent partial-year dip in FY26 is attributed by Parks to facility closures for capital renovations at some centers.

sources.label: NYC Department of Parks and Recreation, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Parks and Recreation, Preliminary Fiscal 2026 Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing as centers recovered from pandemic closures; FY26 dip reflects renovations
